item.title: cmd_review --since flag missing validate_arg for empty value
item.intent: cmd_review must reject --since without a value — silently consuming the next flag as a commit hash causes the review to run with wrong scope, giving users false confidence the review covered the intended range.
item.description: In cmd_review(), the --since flag case at line 2428 does 'since="$2"; shift 2' with no validate_arg call. If the user runs 'aishore review --since' without a commit hash, $2 is either empty or the next flag (e.g. --update-docs). Under set -u, an empty $2 accessed as a positional parameter would cause an unbound variable error; but since $2 is accessed inside a case branch where shift already happened, it just silently sets since to "" or the wrong value. All other value-bearing flags across the codebase call validate_arg first. Compare with cmd_backlog_history --since at lines 4019-4025, which correctly validates the value before using it.
steps: In .aishore/aishore, find cmd_review() and locate the --since case at line 2428
Add validate_arg "$1" "${2:-}" || return 1 before the assignment, matching the pattern used in cmd_backlog_edit()
Verify that 'aishore review --since' (no value) now exits non-zero with an error
Verify that 'aishore review --since abc123' and 'aishore review --since abc --update-docs' still work
acceptanceCriteria: aishore review --since (no value) exits non-zero with a clear error message
aishore review --since <commit> and aishore review --since --update-docs still work correctly
Error message matches the validate_arg pattern used by other flags
